--- title: Repeated-Measures ANOVA subtitle: Sample Problems # author: Guenther, Benjamin A. project: type: website output-dir: docs format: html: html-math-method: mathjax self-contained-math: false embed-resources: false jupyter: python3 --- # About the repeated-measures ANOVA problems use the `RamdomData` class which requires: * an integer value for the number of groups * the sample size per group - all groups will have the same sample size * a call to the `anova()` method with `test = "repeated-measures"` Each of the problems below sets factor levels using a random integer between 2 and 5. Per-group sample size is randomly set between 4 and 10. An example funciton call is included below ::: {#3133ceed .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} n_groups = random.randint(2,5) sample_size = random.randint(4,10) RandomData(groups = n_groups, n = sample_size).anova(test = "repeated-measures") ``` ::: # Problem 1 Given the following within-subjects data, use a repeated-measures ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.05}$ ```{=html}
ID A B C D
1 29 29 26 20
2 32 27 21 19
3 27 28 12 23
4 26 22 18 27
5 31 27 28 28
6 33 30 28 22
7 27 29 26 23
```
Summary statistics for these data:
$G = {718} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {19002} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {28}$
$T_{A} = {205} \quad SS_{A} = {45.43}$
$T_{B} = {192} \quad SS_{B} = {41.71}$
$T_{C} = {159} \quad SS_{C} = {217.43}$
$T_{D} = {162} \quad SS_{D} = {66.86}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {3.16}, \alpha = {0.05}$

Stage 1 Calculations:

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{total} = N - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {28} - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {27}$$

$$df_{between} = k - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {4} - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {3}$$

$$df_{within} = N - K$$
$$df_{within} = {28} - {4}$$
$$df_{within} = {24}$$

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{total} = {19002} - \frac{718^2}{28}$$
$$SS_{total} = {19002} - \frac{515524}{28}$$
$$SS_{total} = {19002} - {18411.57}$$
$$SS_{total} = {590.43}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$
$$SS_{within} = {45.43 + 41.71 + 217.43 + 66.86}$$
$$SS_{within} = {371.43}$$

$$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$$
$$SS_{between} = {590.43} - {371.43}$$
$$SS_{between} = {219.0}$$

note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:
$$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

Stage 2 Calculations:

Partition the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{subjects} = n - 1$$
$$df_{subjects} = {7} - 1$$
$$df_{subjects} = {6}$$

$$df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}$$
$$df_{error} = {24} - {6}$$
$$df_{error} = {18}$$

Calculate person sums and add a new column (*P*) ```{=html}
ID A B C D P
1 29 29 26 20 104
2 32 27 21 19 99
3 27 28 12 23 90
4 26 22 18 27 93
5 31 27 28 28 114
6 33 30 28 22 113
7 27 29 26 23 105
``` $$SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {\frac{104^2}{4} + \frac{99^2}{4} + \frac{90^2}{4} + \frac{93^2}{4} + \frac{114^2}{4} + \frac{113^2}{4} + \frac{105^2}{4}} - \frac{718^2}{28}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {\frac{10816}{4} + \frac{9801}{4} + \frac{8100}{4} + \frac{8649}{4} + \frac{12996}{4} + \frac{12769}{4} + \frac{11025}{4}} - \frac{515524}{28}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {{2704.0} + {2450.25} + {2025.0} + {2162.25} + {3249.0} + {3192.25} + {2756.25}} - {18411.57}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {18539.0} - {18411.57}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {127.43}$$

$$SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}$$
$$SS_{error} = {371.43} - {127.43}$$
$$SS_{error} = {244.0}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
$$MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}$$
$$MS_{between} = \frac{219.0}{3}$$
$$MS_{between} = {73.0}$$

$$MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}$$
$$MS_{error} = \frac{244.0}{18}$$
$$MS_{error} = {13.56}$$

Calculate the *F*-Ratio
$$F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}$$
$$F_{obt} = \frac{73.0}{13.56}$$
$$F_{obt} = {5.38}$$

Calculate $\eta^2$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}$$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{219.0}{{590.43} - {127.43}}$$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{219.0}{463.0}$$
$$\eta_p^2 = {0.47}$$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({3}, {18}) = {5.38}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.47}$ # Problem 2 Given the following within-subjects data, use a repeated-measures ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.05}$ ```{=html}
ID A B
1 49 34
2 61 49
3 37 31
4 68 51
5 71 66
```
Summary statistics for these data:
$G = {517} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {28631} \quad k = {2} \quad N = {10}$
$T_{A} = {286} \quad SS_{A} = {796.8}$
$T_{B} = {231} \quad SS_{B} = {802.8}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {7.71}, \alpha = {0.05}$

Stage 1 Calculations:

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{total} = N - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {10} - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {9}$$

$$df_{between} = k - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {2} - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {1}$$

$$df_{within} = N - K$$
$$df_{within} = {10} - {2}$$
$$df_{within} = {8}$$

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{total} = {28631} - \frac{517^2}{10}$$
$$SS_{total} = {28631} - \frac{267289}{10}$$
$$SS_{total} = {28631} - {26728.9}$$
$$SS_{total} = {1902.1}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$
$$SS_{within} = {796.8 + 802.8}$$
$$SS_{within} = {1599.6}$$

$$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$$
$$SS_{between} = {1902.1} - {1599.6}$$
$$SS_{between} = {302.5}$$

note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:
$$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

Stage 2 Calculations:

Partition the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{subjects} = n - 1$$
$$df_{subjects} = {5} - 1$$
$$df_{subjects} = {4}$$

$$df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}$$
$$df_{error} = {8} - {4}$$
$$df_{error} = {4}$$

Calculate person sums and add a new column (*P*) ```{=html}
ID A B P
1 49 34 83
2 61 49 110
3 37 31 68
4 68 51 119
5 71 66 137
``` $$SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {\frac{83^2}{2} + \frac{110^2}{2} + \frac{68^2}{2} + \frac{119^2}{2} + \frac{137^2}{2}} - \frac{517^2}{10}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {\frac{6889}{2} + \frac{12100}{2} + \frac{4624}{2} + \frac{14161}{2} + \frac{18769}{2}} - \frac{267289}{10}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {{3444.5} + {6050.0} + {2312.0} + {7080.5} + {9384.5}} - {26728.9}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {28271.5} - {26728.9}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {1542.6}$$

$$SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}$$
$$SS_{error} = {1599.6} - {1542.6}$$
$$SS_{error} = {57.0}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
$$MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}$$
$$MS_{between} = \frac{302.5}{1}$$
$$MS_{between} = {302.5}$$

$$MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}$$
$$MS_{error} = \frac{57.0}{4}$$
$$MS_{error} = {14.25}$$

Calculate the *F*-Ratio
$$F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}$$
$$F_{obt} = \frac{302.5}{14.25}$$
$$F_{obt} = {21.23}$$

Calculate $\eta^2$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}$$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{302.5}{{1902.1} - {1542.6}}$$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{302.5}{359.5}$$
$$\eta_p^2 = {0.84}$$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({1}, {4}) = {21.23}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.84}$ # Problem 3 Given the following within-subjects data, use a repeated-measures ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$ ```{=html}
ID A B C
1 40 46 37
2 40 45 36
3 44 52 44
4 60 49 40
5 49 45 42
6 48 36 40
7 39 44 33
8 49 49 40
9 45 55 34
10 36 61 41
```
Summary statistics for these data:
$G = {1319} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {59425} \quad k = {3} \quad N = {30}$
$T_{A} = {450} \quad SS_{A} = {434.0}$
$T_{B} = {482} \quad SS_{B} = {417.6}$
$T_{C} = {387} \quad SS_{C} = {114.1}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {6.01}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Stage 1 Calculations:

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{total} = N - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {30} - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {29}$$

$$df_{between} = k - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {3} - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {2}$$

$$df_{within} = N - K$$
$$df_{within} = {30} - {3}$$
$$df_{within} = {27}$$

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{total} = {59425} - \frac{1319^2}{30}$$
$$SS_{total} = {59425} - \frac{1739761}{30}$$
$$SS_{total} = {59425} - {57992.03}$$
$$SS_{total} = {1432.97}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$
$$SS_{within} = {434.0 + 417.6 + 114.1}$$
$$SS_{within} = {965.7}$$

$$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$$
$$SS_{between} = {1432.97} - {965.7}$$
$$SS_{between} = {467.27}$$

note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:
$$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

Stage 2 Calculations:

Partition the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{subjects} = n - 1$$
$$df_{subjects} = {10} - 1$$
$$df_{subjects} = {9}$$

$$df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}$$
$$df_{error} = {27} - {9}$$
$$df_{error} = {18}$$

Calculate person sums and add a new column (*P*) ```{=html}
ID A B C P
1 40 46 37 123
2 40 45 36 121
3 44 52 44 140
4 60 49 40 149
5 49 45 42 136
6 48 36 40 124
7 39 44 33 116
8 49 49 40 138
9 45 55 34 134

``` $$SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {\frac{123^2}{3} + \frac{121^2}{3} + \frac{140^2}{3} + \frac{149^2}{3} + \frac{136^2}{3} + \frac{124^2}{3} + \frac{116^2}{3} + \frac{138^2}{3} + \frac{134^2}{3} + \frac{138^2}{3}} - \frac{1319^2}{30}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {\frac{15129}{3} + \frac{14641}{3} + \frac{19600}{3} + \frac{22201}{3} + \frac{18496}{3} + \frac{15376}{3} + \frac{13456}{3} + \frac{19044}{3} + \frac{17956}{3} + \frac{19044}{3}} - \frac{1739761}{30}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {{5043.0} + {4880.33} + {6533.33} + {7400.33} + {6165.33} + {5125.33} + {4485.33} + {6348.0} + {5985.33} + {6348.0}} - {57992.03}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {58314.31} - {57992.03}$$

$$SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}$$
$$SS_{error} = {965.7} - {322.28}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
$$MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}$$
$$MS_{between} = \frac{467.27}{2}$$

$$MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}$$

Calculate the *F*-Ratio

Calculate $\eta^2$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}$$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{467.27}{{1432.97} - {322.28}}$$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({2}, {18}) = {6.54}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.42}$ # Problem 4 Given the following within-subjects data, use a repeated-measures ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$ ```{=html}
ID A B C D
1 44 23 36 45
2 42 22 40 41
3 40 41 50 58
4 39 36 46 48
5 37 34 34 50
6 42 29 46 43
```
Summary statistics for these data:
$G = {966} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {40448} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {24}$
$T_{A} = {244} \quad SS_{A} = {31.33}$
$T_{B} = {185} \quad SS_{B} = {282.83}$
$T_{C} = {252} \quad SS_{C} = {200.0}$
$T_{D} = {285} \quad SS_{D} = {185.5}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {5.42}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Stage 1 Calculations:

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{total} = N - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {24} - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {23}$$

$$df_{between} = k - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {4} - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {3}$$

$$df_{within} = N - K$$
$$df_{within} = {24} - {4}$$
$$df_{within} = {20}$$

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{total} = {40448} - \frac{966^2}{24}$$
$$SS_{total} = {40448} - \frac{933156}{24}$$
$$SS_{total} = {40448} - {38881.5}$$
$$SS_{total} = {1566.5}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$
$$SS_{within} = {31.33 + 282.83 + 200.0 + 185.5}$$
$$SS_{within} = {699.66}$$

$$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$$
$$SS_{between} = {1566.5} - {699.66}$$
$$SS_{between} = {866.84}$$

note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:
$$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

Stage 2 Calculations:

Partition the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{subjects} = n - 1$$
$$df_{subjects} = {6} - 1$$
$$df_{subjects} = {5}$$

$$df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}$$
$$df_{error} = {20} - {5}$$
$$df_{error} = {15}$$

Calculate person sums and add a new column (*P*) ```{=html}
ID A B C D P
1 44 23 36 45 148
2 42 22 40 41 145
3 40 41 50 58 189
4 39 36 46 48 169
5 37 34 34 50 155
6 42 29 46 43 160
``` $$SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {\frac{148^2}{4} + \frac{145^2}{4} + \frac{189^2}{4} + \frac{169^2}{4} + \frac{155^2}{4} + \frac{160^2}{4}} - \frac{966^2}{24}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {\frac{21904}{4} + \frac{21025}{4} + \frac{35721}{4} + \frac{28561}{4} + \frac{24025}{4} + \frac{25600}{4}} - \frac{933156}{24}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {{5476.0} + {5256.25} + {8930.25} + {7140.25} + {6006.25} + {6400.0}} - {38881.5}$$
$$SS_{subjects} = {39209.0} - {38881.5}$$

$$SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}$$
$$SS_{error} = {699.66} - {327.5}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
$$MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}$$
$$MS_{between} = \frac{866.84}{3}$$
$$MS_{between} = {288.95}$$

$$MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}$$
$$MS_{error} = \frac{372.16}{15}$$

Calculate the *F*-Ratio
$$F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}$$
$$F_{obt} = \frac{288.95}{24.81}$$

Calculate $\eta^2$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}$$
$$\eta_p^2 = \frac{866.84}{{1566.5} - {327.5}}$$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({3}, {15}) = {11.65}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.7}$ # Problem 5 Given the following within-subjects data, use a repeated-measures ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$ ```{=html}

State the Hypotheses

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$

Partition the Degrees of Freedom

$$df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}$$

Calculate person sums and add a new column (*P*) ```{=html}

$$SS_{subjects} = {\frac{107^2}{3} + \frac{134^2}{3} + \frac{119^2}{3} + \frac{160^2}{3} + \frac{122^2}{3} + \frac{134^2}{3} + \frac{132^2}{3} + \frac{114^2}{3} + \frac{121^2}{3}} - \frac{1143^2}{27}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
